pattern: Once you decide every surviving bag should hold the same count, that shared count is best searched among the counts that already exist in the input, since any other target only wastes more beans.
hints: Any bag can also become empty, so the final shared count only needs to be checked against values that already appear in the input.
Sort the bags so that, for a candidate shared count, every smaller bag is emptied entirely and every larger-or-equal bag is trimmed down to that count.
Total beans removed equals the overall sum minus the shared count times how many bags are large enough to keep it, so pick whichever candidate maximizes that kept amount.

edge_inputs.description: a single bag never needs anything removed
two bags of different sizes, emptying the smaller can beat trimming the larger
one huge outlier among many identical small bags
input already sorted in descending order
single bag at the maximum bound
two bags both at the maximum bound

stress.description: random bag sizes across the full length and value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    beans = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [beans]
